warnings: Overall run_status is warning: all branch-dependent and astroparticle stages (fopt, pta, constraints, prior_audit) carry warning status, propagated to the verdict.
Cosmological viability is conditional/unverified: epsilon=0 was fixed for the whole chain (A01), reheating/BBN require epsilon~1e-6..1e-3.
beta/H convention mismatch (152.7 vs 21.1 at lit Point A; A02) and dbf K_sw->1 template saturation (A03) weaken the spectral-amplitude interpretation.
No Bayes factor / evidence computed (A04); the NANOGrav fit is a goodness-of-region statement, not a model comparison.

Citation-content mismatch: arXiv:2511.15687 is described in upstream handoffs as a 'dbf bulk-flow template' paper but is actually Lewicki & Vaskonen on cosmic-expansion corrections to supercooled-PT spectra. Cited only for its true subject; flagged in report Sec. Inconsistencies.
approximations: Report reproduces upstream numbers verbatim from handoff JSONs; no new physics computed.
FOPT high-T bounce, Gaussian percolation, mu=w (no RG/Daisy), neglected scalar/Goldstone.
dbf bulk-flow template with runaway walls vw->1; T=Treh redshift; K_sw saturated.

cited_sources.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector
Supercooled phase transitions in conformal dark sectors explain NANOGrav data
Supercooled Dark Scalar Phase Transitions explanation of NANOGrav data
Gravitational waves from a dark U(1)_D phase transition in the light of NANOGrav 12.5 yr data
NANOGrav Signal from a Dark Conformal Phase Transition
Revisiting Supernova 1987A Constraints on Dark Photons
Impact of cosmic expansion on gravitational wave spectra from strongly supercooled first-order phase transitions

inconsistencies.description: Upstream handoff_pta.json and handoff_prior_audit.json attribute the 'dbf bulk-flow' GW template / 'expansion-corrected supercooled template' to arXiv:2511.15687. The arXiv ID exists but is 'Impact of cosmic expansion on gravitational wave spectra from strongly supercooled first-order phase transitions' by M. Lewicki & V. Vaskonen, which is about cosmic-expansion corrections, not a dedicated dissipative bulk-flow template. Cited only for its actual subject; attribution caveat documented in report.
affected_outputs: bibliographic attribution of the dbf template

inconsistencies.description: FOPT backend reports beta/H ~ 152.7 at literature Point A vs 21.1 in arXiv:2502.19478 (factor ~7). Reported as prior-audit item A02; an internal convention issue, not a cross-handoff data conflict.

unverified_or_rejected_references.note: Exists and is cited for its true content; the upstream 'dbf template' description does not match the paper.
